metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4177
metadata.comment:
Rationale: This publication provides evidence that ELKS1 is localized to the presynapse but is not restricted to the active zone and distributed throughout the nerve terminal.

-STED microscopy was used to assess the subsynaptic localization of ELKS1 (Figures 3E-3K) and established conditional double KO (cDKO) of ELKS1alpha and ELKS2alpha (cDKO) were used as negative controls (Figures S3H and S3I) (Held et al., 2016; Liu et al., 2014). Bassoon was an active zone marker within a synapse. In side-view STED images of synapses, ELKS1 was widely distributed throughout nerve terminals with no clear peak, and its mild average peak was shifted. This is in contrast to proteins confined to the active zone which have their peaks precisely colocalized with Bassoon (Figures 3H-3J).
- As second way of analyzing this by two separate ROIs - synaptic and active zone defined by Synapsin-1 and Bassoon, respectively - were used. The fraction of synaptic ELKS1 that was not at the active zone was quantified (Figure 3K). This also demonstrated that the majority of ELKS1 was away from the active zone.. Experimental description: Primary mouse hippocampal cultures were prepared from newborn pups (P0-P1) of either sex on glass coverslips in 24-well plates. For all experiments, neuronal cultures were harvested at DIV14-16 and were imaged using STED microscopy.
